summary: No-ship: the locked RED witness is valid, but P-4 remains acceptance-bypassable. Simpler alternative: mechanically pin the production wrapper’s exact delegation instead of adding another hook.

findings.title: W11 can pass while production recovery remains broken
findings.body: W11 calls `recover_graves_from` directly, while W8 scans only `reconcile.rs`. The correct helper can therefore exist solely to satisfy W11 while production `recover_graves` delegates to an old-style raw loop moved into `reconcile/entry.rs`. W8 sees no prohibited strings, W11 and normal recovery characterizations pass, yet `PassGuard::begin` still aborts when a snapshotted grave disappears. The claimed closure of P-4/M14 is therefore not enforced at the real production entrypoint.
findings.recommendation: Add a source/AST invariant pinning `recover_graves` to exactly snapshot then call `recover_graves_from`, with exactly one production helper call. Also mutation-check the dead-helper/raw-production variant or run W11 through a deterministic seam on the actual production entrypoint.

next_steps: Strengthen P-4 acceptance to bind W11’s tested helper to the production `PassGuard::begin` path, then repeat the scoped review.
